what_it_does: Defines the module constitution for panels/ — declarative UI panel definitions (JSON field specs) that bind fields to real config values, are authored by the brain through runtime operations, rendered by canvas, and must be operator-approved and git-committed.
observations: Frontmatter marks it as a living prescriptive constitution for the panels module.
Panels are declared as pure data with no code, binding fields to real config values.
The registry overrides any guessed select-options with real registered values.
New panels require operator approval and are applied via propose_panel/apply_panel, then git-committed.
It explicitly positions panels as the middle tier of self-modification between nodes and extensions.
It forbids hand-authoring select-options that should come from the registry and forbids applying panels without operator approval.
It refuses to enumerate the live panel set, pointing to Company Map as the single source of truth.
standouts: The 'others' tier of self-modification framing: nodes / panels / extensions.
Registry-driven select options as a path-of-least-resistance anti-drift mechanism.
Git-reversibility guarantee for applying a panel.
Operator approval gate for brain-authored changes.
conventions: Uses a constitution frontmatter with type, register, module, aliases, tags, governs, relates-to, and status.
Declares a single source of truth and avoids enumerating live data locally.
Separates data (panels) from behavior (extensions) and rendering (canvas).
Requires operator approval and git commit for mutations.
notes: This is the governance doc for declarative UI panels, defining a reversible, operator-approved, registry-backed self-modification tier between node types and code extensions.
questions: What is the exact JSON schema for a panel definition?
What do propose_panel and apply_panel do mechanically?
What is the 'brain' and what is the 'RHM'?
How does canvas generically render panel fields?
What is the registry and how does it resolve option values?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it explains what panels are, how they are authored, rendered, extended, and constrained, and aligns with the described guarantees and seams.
apparent_themes: Registry-backed option binding to prevent drift
intention_signals: To create a safe, low-code self-modification layer where UI panels can be proposed as declarative data, bound to real config, kept in sync with the registry, and rolled back if needed.
novelty: The explicit 'panels' tier between node types and extensions, plus the registry overriding guessed select options as the default path-of-least-resistance behavior.
contribution: Defines the panels tier and its guarantees, separating declarative UI config from code extensions, and enforces registry-backed option binding plus operator-approved, git-committed changes.
summary_for_embedding: company/panels/AGENTS.md is a living module constitution that defines declarative UI panels as JSON field definitions binding to real config values, authored via runtime, rendered by canvas, and governed by operator-approved, git-reversible, registry-backed rules.
intention_for_embedding: To establish a safe, reversible, low-code self-modification tier for brain-authored UI panels situated between node types and extensions.
